workflow_memory.content:
You are the chat assistant for Eco_RAG.
Be clear, grounded, and concise. If you are unsure, say so.

You must follow this workflow protocol for every assistant decision reply:
- On the first decision for a user turn, reply with a `[plan]` block.
- After any tool result, continue the same workflow and reply with a `[think]` block.
- In the same reply, choose exactly one next step with `[next]`.
- `[next]` must be either `retrieve` or `answer`.
- If `[next]` is `retrieve`, include exactly one `[retrieve]` block with one safe function call.
- If `[next]` is `answer`, include exactly one `[answer]` block with the final user-facing answer.
- Return plain text only. Do not use provider-native tool calls.

Allowed retrieve tools:
load_skill, database_search, web_search

Available skills:
- database_search
- web_search

Safe retrieve command examples:
- `web_search("Eco")`
- `web_search(["Eco project", "Eco person"])`
- `database_search("workflow graph", top_k=4)`
- `load_skill("database_search")`

Output formats:
[plan]
<brief reasoning>
[next]
retrieve
[retrieve]
<one safe command>

[plan]
<brief reasoning>
[next]
answer
[answer]
<final answer>

[think]
<brief reasoning over the accumulated tool results>
[next]
retrieve
[retrieve]
<one safe command>

[think]
<brief reasoning over the accumulated tool results>
[next]
answer
[answer]
<final answer>

[plan]
The user said "hi". This is a simple greeting, so I should respond with a friendly greeting in return. No retrieval is needed.

[think]
The web search results show that "Ecokayiza" is a GitHub username associated with a repository called "Wallpaper_Manager". The user also has a personal website (ecokayizasweb.xyz) with content about Transformer models. This appears to be an individual developer or tech enthusiast, likely focused on software tools (like wallpaper management) and possibly machine learning topics. The information from the search is sufficient to answer the question.

[plan]
The user is asking about the projects of "Ecokayiza." From the previous conversation, we know Ecokayiza is a GitHub user. To answer this, I need to retrieve more specific information about their projects, likely from their GitHub profile or other online sources. A web search for their projects would be appropriate.

[next]
retrieve
[retrieve]
web_search("Ecokayiza projects GitHub")

[tool]
web_search(query='Ecokayiza projects GitHub')

1. ecokayiza (Ecoka) · GitHub
URL: https://github.com/ecokayiza
I'm Ecokayiza — passionate about NLP, LLMs, Agents, and building open-source tools.🤓👆 🔬 Currently learning Deep Learning (DL)🖐️😭🤚 🛠️ Always tinkering with little projects 💦 Feel free to reach out: ecokayiza@gmail.com

2. Fifth Element Star Milla Jovovich Reveals AI Memory Tool MemPalace
URL: https://decrypt.co/363524/fifth-element-milla-jovovich-ai-tool-mempalace
Milla Jovovich, the Fifth Element and Resident Evil star, reveals an AI project inspired by the ancient "memory palace" method.

3. ecokayiza/README.md at main - GitHub
URL: https://github.com/ecokayiza/ecokayiza/blob/main/README.md
Contribute to ecokayiza / ecokayiza development by creating an account on GitHub .

4. ecokayiza (Ecoka) / Repositories · GitHub
URL: https://github.com/ecokayiza?tab=repositories
The ultimate goal of AI is all about creating my waifu.（😡👍🏻） - ecokayiza

5. GitHub - ecokayiza/ecokayiza
URL: https://github.com/ecokayiza/ecokayiza
I'm Ecokayiza — passionate about NLP, LLMs, Agents, and building open-source tools.🤓👆 🔬 Currently learning Deep Learning (DL)🖐️😭🤚 🛠️ Always tinkering with little projects 💦 Feel free to reach out: ecokayiza@gmail.com

Continue the same workflow using the full transcript above, including the tool result you just received.
Use a `[think]` block for this continuation.
Retrieval still allowed: yes
Available retrieve tools: load_skill, database_search, web_search

Return plain text only.
If more external information is needed and retrieval is still allowed, reply with:
[think]
<brief reasoning>
[next]
retrieve
[retrieve]
<one safe command>

Otherwise reply with:
[think]
<brief reasoning>
[next]
answer
[answer]
<final answer>

logs.message: web_search requires one or more query strings.
